data.title: Treasure Global Launches $100 Million Digital Asset Finance Strategy
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: E-commerce platform operator Treasure Global Inc. (NASDAQ: TGL) announced the launch of a $100 million digital asset fund to strengthen its digital infrastructure and support the launch of an AI consumer intelligence platform. The program is funded in two parts: $50 million from existing equity financing agreements and another $50 million from new capital injections from strategic institutional investors. The funds will be used in stages to allocate blockchain native assets such as Bitcoin, Ethereum and regulated stablecoins, aiming to improve capital efficiency and lay the foundation for future tokenization, loyalty programs and blockchain settlement systems. (GlobeNewswire)

data.title: U.S. May PMI Data Exceeds Expectations
data.description: According to Odaily, the final reading for the U.S. S&P Global Services PMI in May was 53.7, surpassing the expected 52.3 and the previous value of 52.3. Additionally, the final U.S. S&P Global Composite PMI for May reached 53, exceeding the forecast of 52.1 and the prior figure of 52.1. Following the release of the PMI data, there was minimal short-term fluctuation in the spot prices of gold and silver, as well as the U.S. Dollar Index (DXY).

data.title: Binance to Launch Spot Altcoin LiquidityBoost Program
data.description:
According to Binance's official announcement, the cryptocurrency exchange is set to introduce the Spot Altcoin LiquidityBoost Program. This initiative aims to enhance liquidity and trading efficiency for selected altcoin trading pairs on the Spot market. The program is designed to offer users tighter spreads, reduced slippage, and an overall improved trading experience. The qualification period for this program will begin on 2025-06-09 00:00 (UTC).

**Program Details**

- Liquidity providers will undergo weekly reviews based on a performance review mechanism.

- To qualify for Tier 1 rebate rates, users must achieve a combined Maker Volume Percentage of 0.5% against any of the eligible altcoin spot trading pairs.

- For Tier 2 rebate rates, a combined Maker Volume Percentage of 1.0% is required.

- The Maker Fee Rebate Rate is set at -0.005% for Tier 1 and -0.010% for Tier 2.

- Maker fee rebate rates will be updated weekly starting from 2025-06-17 00:00 (UTC).

- Accounts qualifying for both the Spot Liquidity Provider Program and the Altcoin LiquidityBoost Program will receive the more favorable fee rate for traded pairs.

**Eligible Altcoin Spot Trading Pairs**

- The program includes trading pairs such as INITUSDT, EOSUSDT, HYPERUSDT, PARTIUSDT, ICPUSDT, KERNELUSDT, CFXUSDT, WUSDT, KMNOUSDT, IOTXUSDT, ONDOUSDT, TONUSDT, FILUSDT, WCTUSDT, BABYUSDT, SXTUSDT, SYRUPUSDT, and STOUSDT.

**Participation Requirements**

- Users with a 30-day trading volume exceeding 20,000,000 USDT equivalent on Binance Spot and Margin, or as evidenced across other platforms, are invited to join.

- Interested participants must provide proof of trading volume to mmprogram@binance.com or contact their sales or key account coverage team.

**Additional Notes**

- The program is limited to specified altcoin trading pairs on Binance.com.

- Maker rebates will be settled in USDT.

- Rebate eligibility is based on weekly performance across eligible altcoin pairs.

- Maker Fee Rebates are effective from 00:00 (UTC) each Tuesday to 23:59 (UTC) the following Monday and are paid hourly.

- Users can check their rebate history via Orders > Transaction History > Distribution > Fiat and Spot.

- Participants in multiple liquidity programs will receive the more favorable rebate ratio.

- Users failing to qualify for rebates for three consecutive weeks will be removed from the program.

Binance reserves the right to amend or terminate the program at its discretion, considering factors such as regulatory changes, technical issues, or the need to protect users and the platform. For more details, users can refer to the VIP and Institutional Services page. Discrepancies between the original English content and translations may occur, and the English version should be considered the most accurate.

data.title: Nobel Laureates Urge Senate to Reject House Tax and Spending Bill
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, six Nobel Prize-winning economists have expressed concern over a tax and spending bill recently passed by House Republicans. The economists have issued an open letter highlighting their worries about significant cuts to Medicaid and the Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program (SNAP). They argue that these programs are vital for American families.

The House version of the bill proposes a $700 billion reduction in Medicaid spending and a $300 billion cut to SNAP, marking the largest reductions in the history of both programs. The economists are urging the Senate to reject the bill and to start anew in crafting the budget.

data.title: U.S. Stock Market Opens with Slight Gains as Cryptocurrency Stocks Decline
data.description: According to BlockBeats, the U.S. stock market opened with modest gains across its three major indices. However, cryptocurrency-related stocks experienced slight declines. MicroStrategy saw a decrease of 0.95%, MARA Holdings fell by 1.08%, and Riot Platforms dropped by 1%. The market movements reflect ongoing volatility in the cryptocurrency sector.

data.title: The final value of the US S&P Global Services PMI in May was 53.7, while the expected value was 52.3
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News: The final value of the US S&P Global Services PMI in May was 53.7, expected to be 52.3, and the previous value was 52.3.
The final value of the US S&P Global Composite PMI in May was 53, expected to be 52.1, and the previous value was 52.1.
After the release of the US PMI data, the short-term fluctuations of spot gold and silver and the US dollar index DXY were not large. (Jinshi)

data.title: Gate delists LAUSDT perpetual contract, and the platform bears all losses caused by liquidation
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Because the contract mark price deviated seriously from the normal market price, Gate announced on June 4 that it would delist the LAUSDT perpetual contract and suspend related transactions. The platform has closed all open orders at market prices and promised that the platform will bear all losses from negative balances, and users do not need to cover their positions. The official said that this move is aimed at maintaining a fair trading environment and the security of user assets.

data.title: Binance Alpha Lists POKT
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News According to the official page, Binance Alpha launched POKT, with a market value of US$2.05 million at the time of launch.

data.title: Moscow Exchange’s BTC futures contracts will be quoted in USD and settled in rubles
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News According to Russian media Tass, the Moscow Exchange plans to officially launch Bitcoin futures trading on June 4, 2025. This product is only open to qualified investors. The first batch of futures contracts will expire in September 2025. The contract is quoted in US dollars and settled in rubles based on the price of the Bitcoin Trust ETF. Previously, the Russian Central Bank approved financial institutions to provide cryptocurrency-linked financial derivatives to qualified investors in May, but recommended avoiding direct investment in cryptocurrencies. (Tass)

data.title: Moscow Exchange to Launch Bitcoin Futures for Qualified Investors in June 2025
data.description: According to Odaily, Moscow Exchange is set to introduce Bitcoin futures trading on June 4, 2025. This offering will be available exclusively to qualified investors. The initial batch of futures contracts is scheduled to expire in September 2025, quoted in U.S. dollars and settled in rubles, based on the price of a Bitcoin trust ETF. Previously, the Russian Central Bank had approved financial institutions to offer cryptocurrency-linked derivatives to qualified investors in May, while advising against direct investment in cryptocurrencies.

data.title: Australia Imposes Limits on Crypto ATM Transactions to Combat Elderly Fraud
data.description:
According to Foresight News, CoinDesk reports that Australia's anti-money laundering regulator, AUSTRAC, has implemented restrictions on cryptocurrency ATM providers due to concerns over elderly fraud. The new measures include a $5,000 limit on cash deposits and withdrawals at crypto ATMs. Operators are required to enhance their customer due diligence obligations, which involve issuing scam warnings and monitoring transactions.

Regulatory data indicates that crypto ATMs are frequently used for scam-related transactions, with scammers often targeting older individuals. Users aged 50 and above account for 72% of all transactions, while those aged 60 to 70 make up 29% of the transactions. These measures aim to protect vulnerable populations from financial exploitation.

data.title: BlackRock has increased its holdings of 214,000 ETH since May 11, worth $560 million
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News According to Lookonchain monitoring, although the ETH trading price has been consolidating between US$2,500 and US$2,700, BlackRock has increased its holdings of 214,000 ETH, worth US$560 million, since May 11.

data.title: WazirX exits Singapore and moves to Panama
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News WazirX, a cryptocurrency exchange serving India but headquartered in Singapore, announced that it would move its operational headquarters to Panama and rename its parent company Zettai to Zensui. This move was made after the Singapore High Court refused to approve its restructuring plan.
It is reported that Zensui was incorporated in Panama on March 10 and is expected to take over WazirX's cryptocurrency-related services within a few days. In addition, Zensui will be responsible for issuing "recovery tokens" related to compensation for the approximately US$235 million hacking incident in July 2024, which is designed to provide affected users with 75% to 80% of their account balances. WazirX said it does not intend to apply for relevant licenses in Singapore or India, and the move is seen as a response to Singapore's increasingly stringent crypto regulatory policies. (Cointelegraph)

data.title: Circle's IPO Oversubscribed by Over 25 Times
data.description: According to BlockBeats, Circle, the issuer of the USDC stablecoin, has seen its initial public offering (IPO) oversubscribed by more than 25 times, as reported by Bloomberg. This significant interest highlights the strong demand for Circle's shares in the market.

data.title: BounceBit: Now supports the stablecoin USD1 issued by WLFI
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News BounceBit officially announced that it now supports the institutional-grade stablecoin USD1 issued by WLFI. With this integration, USD1 has now become an eligible asset in the BounceBit CeDeFi portal. Users can directly deploy USD1 into the "automatic" strategy, which directs funds to centralized and decentralized platforms for secure, Delta-neutral income opportunities.

data.title: Berachain plans to use Ethereum’s Pectra upgrade strategy for “Bectra Upgrade”
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News Layer1 blockchain Berachain announced that it has become a non-Ethereum chain that fully implements the Ethereum Pectra execution layer function through the "Bectra" hard fork. The Bectra upgrade enables user wallets to have smart account functions, support batch transactions, set consumption limits, use stablecoin HONEY to pay gas fees and regular payments, etc. Previously, these functions had to rely on custom contracts or third-party implementations. (CoinDesk)

data.title: Market News: Circle's IPO is oversubscribed more than 25 times
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News Market News: The pricing of the initial public offering (IPO) of stablecoin giant Circle is reportedly likely to be higher than the market pricing range. It is reported that Circle's IPO was oversubscribed by more than 25 times. (Jinshi)
